page: Microsoft Azure L32s is an LS family virtual machine instance running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor based on the x86_64 architecture, providing 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor includes a 40 MB L3 cache and supports AVX, AES, BMI2, and FMA instruction sets, though it lacks AVX-512 capabilities. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core.

A key feature of the L32s instance is its substantial local storage capacity, offering 5765 GB of Premium Storage capable capacity. This high storage density makes it suitable for data-intensive workloads. However, benchmark data reveals distinct performance tradeoffs. Single-core performance is a notable bottleneck, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance scoring in the bottom 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the bottom 25%. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also rank in the bottom 25%.

Conversely, multi-threaded performance is significantly stronger. The instance achieves average ratings in multi-core stress-ng, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests. Notably, bzip3 multi-threaded decompression ranks in the top 25%, and LLM inference prompt processing for a small 135M model is also in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth and database operations, such as Redis SET operations, consistently perform in the average tier. This hardware profile is best suited for multi-threaded database workloads, large-scale storage operations, and parallel data processing tasks where local storage capacity and memory density outweigh single-core execution speed.
description: The Microsoft Azure L32s is an LS family virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor with 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of RAM and 5765 GB of Premium Storage capable local storage. While single-core benchmarks, such as stress-ng and Geekbench single-core, fall into the bottom 10% and 25% respectively, the instance demonstrates solid multi-threaded capabilities. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently score in the average tier, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and small-model LLM prompt processing reach the top 25%. This configuration offers high storage and memory density, making it suitable for multi-threaded database workloads, large-scale data processing, and storage-heavy applications where parallel execution can offset lower single-core clock speeds.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L32s is an LS family instance with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 5765 GB local storage, offering average multi-core performance but weaker single-core speeds for database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L32s features 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 5765 GB local storage, optimized for multi-threaded database and storage workloads.
tagline: A storage-dense virtual machine featuring 256 GB of RAM and 5.7 TB of local capacity for parallelized database workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 physical cores running at 2.0 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
5765 GB of Premium Storage capable local storage capacity
Strong multi-threaded performance in decompression and small-model LLM prompt processing
Weaker single-core performance across synthetic and compression benchmarks
Suited for multi-threaded databases, parallel data processing, and storage-heavy tasks
